design_decisions.d0r_pending_approval: D0.R made 2 assumption + 0 definition change(s) PROVISIONALLY (pending approval, direction-of-truth align/correct/discharge): ass:ccot-gamma-hadamard, ass:ccot-donsker

added_assumptions.label: ass:ccot-active-face-hadamard-smoothness
added_assumptions.statement: There exist a local active-face neighborhood U_P of P, a seminorm ||.||_F on signed law perturbations, a measurable boundary derivative f_B(o)=chi_P(o)B(o)^(-1/2)1{0<B(o)<=bar_b}, and a deterministic modulus eta_P(s)->0 as s downarrow 0 such that for every law Q in U_P, Gamma(Q)-Gamma(P)=(Q-P)phi_P+(Q-P)f_B+Rem_P(Q) with |Rem_P(Q)|<=||Q-P||_F eta_P(||Q-P||_F).

added_assumptions.label: ass:ccot-empirical-process-scale
added_assumptions.statement: With the neighborhood U_P, seminorm ||.||_F, and modulus eta_P from ass:ccot-active-face-hadamard-smoothness, P(hat_P_n in U_P)->1 and ||hat_P_n-P||_F=O_P(rho_n(alpha)^(-1)).

added_assumptions.label: ass:iid
added_assumptions.statement: O_1, ..., O_n are i.i.d. draws from P.

added_assumptions.label: ass:boundary-tail
added_assumptions.statement: There exists c_B > 0 such that P(0 < B <= t) = c_B t^alpha {1 + o(1)} as t downarrow 0.

added_assumptions.label: ass:boundary-mark-stabilization
added_assumptions.statement: There exists a probability measure M_P on R x A such that the conditional law of (chi_P(O), a_P(O)) given 0 < B <= t converges weakly to M_P as t downarrow 0.

added_assumptions.label: ass:centered-shell-variance
added_assumptions.statement: There exist c_V_minus > 0 and c_V_plus > 0 such that c_V_minus K_alpha(t) <= Var_P( chi_P(O) B^(-1/2) 1{ t < B <= bar_b } ) <= c_V_plus K_alpha(t) for every t in (0, bar_b].

added_assumptions.label: ass:unique-active-face
added_assumptions.statement: For P-almost every X and every realized boundary point with 0 < B <= bar_b, the lower-endpoint CCOT program has a unique active transport face.

added_assumptions.label: ass:dual-gap-scale
added_assumptions.statement: There exists c_gap > 0 such that, on the region 0 < B <= bar_b, the dual value gap between the active face and the closest competing face is at least c_gap B.

added_assumptions.label: ass:stable-shell-domain
added_assumptions.statement: If 0 < alpha < 1, then for every fixed u_0 in (0,1] and every finite set u_1, ..., u_m in [u_0,1], there exists a Levy measure Lambda_P,alpha,u0 on R^(m+1) \ {0} such that n P( ( n^(-1) J_n,P(O), n^(-1) G_n,P,u_1(O), ..., n^(-1) G_n,P,u_m(O) ) in E ) -> Lambda_P,alpha,u0(E) for every Borel set E bounded away from zero, and the centered array has negligible Gaussian part.

added_assumptions.label: ass:regular-linearization
added_assumptions.statement: For perturbations of P supported on the interior region { B > bar_b / 2 }, the endpoint map Gamma is pathwise differentiable with influence function phi_P in L2(P).

added_assumptions.label: ass:strict-overlap-boundary-regularity
added_assumptions.statement: There exists C_so < infinity such that E_P[ chi_P(O)^2 B^(-1) 1{ 0 < B <= t } ] <= C_so for every t in (0, bar_b].

added_assumptions.label: ass:fixed-alpha-calibrator
added_assumptions.statement: For each fixed alpha, the data-driven law estimator hat L_n,alpha in the conditional CI construction satisfies sup_{P in P_n(alpha)} sup_t |hat L_n,alpha((-infinity,t])-P_P(T_n,alpha<=t)| -> 0 in probability at continuity points of the limiting law, its delta/2 and 1-delta/2 quantiles are uniformly consistent, and E_P[hat q_+(alpha)-hat q_-(alpha)] is uniformly O(1).

added_assumptions.label: ass:strict-overlap-empirical-smoothness
added_assumptions.statement: There exist a strict-overlap neighborhood N_P of P, a class F_P of square-integrable measurable functions, a seminorm ||Q-P||_{F_P}=sup_{f in F_P}|(Q-P)f| on signed perturbations of P, and a deterministic modulus eta_P(s) with eta_P(s) -> 0 as s downarrow 0 such that phi_P belongs to F_P, E_P phi_P(O)=0, E_P phi_P(O)^2<infinity, ||hat_P_n-P||_{F_P}=O_P(n^(-1/2)), and for every law Q in N_P, |Gamma(Q)-Gamma(P)-(Q-P)phi_P| <= ||Q-P||_{F_P} eta_P(||Q-P||_{F_P}).

added_assumptions.label: ass:gaussian-shell-lindeberg
added_assumptions.statement: If alpha >= 1, then for every fixed u_0 in (0,1] there exists a continuous positive-semidefinite kernel Sigma_P,u0 on [u_0,1]^2 such that for every finite set u_1, ..., u_m in [u_0,1], n^(-1) Cov_P( G_n,P,u_j(O), G_n,P,u_k(O) ) -> Sigma_P,u0(u_j,u_k) for all j,k and the triangular array { n^(-1/2) [ G_n,P,u_j(O_i) - E_P G_n,P,u_j(O) ] : i <= n } satisfies the multivariate Lindeberg condition.

added_assumptions.label: ass:strict-overlap-propensity
added_assumptions.statement: underline_p(X) >= p_so almost surely.

added_assumptions.label: ass:shell-row-lindeberg
added_assumptions.statement: For alpha>=1, every fixed u_0 in (0,1] and every finite u_1,...,u_m in [u_0,1], the row variables n^(-1)(G_n,P,u_j(O_i)-E_P G_n,P,u_j(O))_{j=1}^m satisfy the multivariate Lindeberg condition, and their row-sum covariance n^(-1)Cov_P(G_n,P,u_j,G_n,P,u_k) converges to Sigma_P,u0(u_j,u_k).

added_assumptions.label: ass:shell-increment-moment
added_assumptions.statement: For every fixed u_0 in (0,1], there exist q>2, beta>1, and C<infinity such that sup_n E_P|H_nP_u0(v)-H_nP_u0(u)|^q <= C|v-u|^beta for all u,v in [u_0,1], and sup_n E_P|H_nP_u0(u_0)|^q <= C.

added_assumptions.label: ass:stable-shell-drift
added_assumptions.statement: If 0<alpha<1, then for every fixed u_0 and finite u_1,...,u_m in [u_0,1], the compensated drift terms for the centered row array n^(-1)(J_n,P,G_n,P,u_1,...,G_n,P,u_m) converge for a common bounded truncation function.

added_assumptions.label: ass:stable-shell-nondegenerate-jump
added_assumptions.statement: If 0<alpha<1, the projection of Lambda_P,alpha,u0 onto the extreme-shell coordinate is nonzero for every fixed u_0 in (0,1].

added_assumptions.label: ass:extreme-shell-first-moment
added_assumptions.statement: For alpha>=1, there are C<infinity and t_0>0 such that E_P[|chi_P(O)| B^(-1/2) 1{0<B<=t}] <= C t^(alpha-1/2) for all 0<t<=t_0.

added_assumptions.label: ass:boundary-annulus-oscillation
added_assumptions.statement: When alpha=1, for every sufficiently small delta_0>0 there are sequences u_n,v_n in (0,delta_0] and constants eta,epsilon>0 such that liminf_n P_P(|H_nP_delta0(u_n)-H_nP_delta0(v_n)|>eta)>=epsilon.

added_assumptions.label: ass:regular-boundary-joint-limit
added_assumptions.statement: If alpha>1, the joint row array formed by n^(-1/2)phi_P(O_i) and n^(-1)(G_n,P,u_j(O_i)-E_PG_n,P,u_j(O)) for every finite u_1,...,u_m satisfies Lindeberg and has a finite limiting covariance matrix.

added_assumptions.label: ass:regular-influence-centering
added_assumptions.statement: The regular influence function is chosen in its canonical centered version: E_P phi_P(O)=0 and E_P phi_P(O)^2<infinity; if a pathwise derivative representative is uncentered, replace it by phi_P-P phi_P without changing (hat P_n-P)phi_P.

added_assumptions.label: ass:irregular-endpoint-projection-nondegeneracy
added_assumptions.statement: If 0<alpha<1, then for every fixed u_0 in (0,1], the projection of Lambda_P,alpha,u0 under the scalar endpoint map that sends the extreme-shell and u=1 truncated-shell coordinates to R+H(1) is a nonzero Levy measure after deterministic centering.

added_assumptions.label: ass:ccot-gamma-hadamard
added_assumptions.statement: The CCOT lower-endpoint functional Gamma is Hadamard differentiable at P tangentially to the relevant model tangent set, with derivative dotGamma_P(h)=integral phi_P(o) dh(o) + integral chi_P(o)B(o)^(-1/2)1{0<B(o)<=bar_b} dh(o).

added_assumptions.label: ass:ccot-donsker
added_assumptions.statement: The empirical fluctuation hat_P_n-P is asymptotically tangential for the Hadamard derivative of Gamma; in particular sqrt(n)(hat_P_n-P) converges weakly in the relevant Donsker tangent space, so the empirical path is admissible for the functional delta method at every normalization rho_n(alpha)<=sqrt(n).

proposed_from.topic: Sharp limit experiment and a tail-adaptive honest-CI adaptation lower bound for the continuous-instrument optimal-transport PRTE sharp partial-identification bound endpoint, indexed by the propensity-boundary tail exponent alpha; phase transition alpha*=1 separating root-n-regular (Gaussian) from boundary-irregular (non-Gaussian) limits; deliver both the boundary-localized limit law and a two-alpha Le Cam adaptation lower bound (no honest CI adapts across the tail class). Anchor: arXiv:2604.12263 (PRTE partial ID with IV via optimal transport), whose Thm 5.11 gives only a rate for the continuous-instrument bound endpoint, no limit law or CI.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:unique-active-face and ass:dual-gap-scale
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Active-face uniqueness and first-order dual-gap separation are imposed as subclass restrictions, not derived from CCOT primitives.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All endpoint limit-law, local-experiment, and adaptation claims are conditional on the unique-active-face, separated-dual-gap subclass.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:gaussian-shell-lindeberg and ass:stable-shell-domain
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The finite-dimensional Gaussian or Levy shell domain-of-attraction objects are assumed as high-level limit regularity conditions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The local-experiment and endpoint-law claims are conditional on the supplied shell CLT/domain-of-attraction conditions and are field-tier unless the shell law is derived from more primitive boundary regular variation.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:boundary-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The positive fixed-alpha honest CI is only a named construction route, not a claimed theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposal claims a limit-law and no-adaptation lower-bound kernel, with fixed-alpha operational CI construction left open.

banked_reason: D0.5 revise-cap exhausted (rubric PASS=field-novel; math NO-PASS). Sound, novel alpha-indexed OT-boundary continuous-instrument PRTE endpoint limit law + alpha*=1 phase transition; the ONE genuine open gap is the boundary-scale plug-in remainder rate (discharge_or_rescope@ass:ccot-donsker) — the OT endpoint's boundary-singular remainder needs OT-functional differentiability theory beyond standard Hadamard+Donsker (a research problem of its own). Cross-alpha adaptation converse honestly refuted (proven unattainable under the class defs) -> OEQ. Reraisable when the OT-differentiability substrate exists.
